message:
fix(diagnose): honor path_topology config; stop hardcoding /data/projects in errors

Root cause: `rch diagnose` and the `rch exec` post-hook entry point both called
`extract_project_name()` — a helper that unconditionally normalized the cwd
against `PathTopologyPolicy::default()` (compiled-in `/data/projects` + `/dp`).
Even when operators set `path_topology.canonical_root` / `alias_root` in
`config.toml`, normalization inside `extract_project_name` continued to use the
defaults, producing the spurious first-line warning:

    Project path normalization failed for <cwd>: canonical root is missing
    (input: <cwd>, detail: missing root /data/projects)

The `status.rs::diagnose` command separately called `normalize_project_path(...)`
(also default-policy) when computing `normalized_project_root`, compounding the
problem.

Fix:
- Add `extract_project_name_with_policy(&PathTopologyPolicy)` and make the
  existing parameterless `extract_project_name()` delegate to it with the
  default policy (preserved for tests/back-compat).
- `rch diagnose` now builds `config.path_topology.to_policy()` at entry and
  threads that policy through both `normalize_project_path_with_policy(...)` and
  `extract_project_name_with_policy(...)`.
- `run_exec` (the post-hook remote execution entry in `hook.rs`) now also
  computes the topology policy from the loaded config and uses it for the
  project-name derivation, eliminating the redundant second
  `to_policy()` call immediately before `execute_remote_compilation`.
- Drop the now-unused default-policy import in `hook.rs`.

Regression tests:
- `rch-common::path_topology::tests::canonical_root_missing_error_cites_configured_root_not_default`
  — constructs a `PathTopologyPolicy` with a missing *custom* root and asserts
  the resulting `PathNormalizationError` Display output mentions the configured
  root and crucially does NOT contain the string `/data/projects`.
- `rch::hook::tests::test_extract_project_name_honors_custom_policy` — creates
  a temp canonical root, cds into a subdir, and asserts
  `extract_project_name_with_policy(&custom)` returns a name derived from the
  configured topology rather than falling back to `unknown` (which is what
  would happen under the default policy on a machine without `/data/projects`).

Closes #9

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
